definition: Setup effort, cognitive load, amount of glue code, and operational complexity for a practical prototype.
Release history, ecosystem traction, project age, and production posture.
Ability to use multiple model providers or local/open-weight models without a hard platform lock-in.
Strength and clarity of execution isolation for agent-written commands and untrusted code.
Native support for sessions, memory, durable state, context offloading, or resumability.
Support for subagents, parallel agents, supervisors, or orchestration across workstreams.
Human-in-the-loop approvals, review loops, steering, and interruptibility.
CI, GitHub, issue, branch, and pull-request automation fit.
Logs, traces, telemetry, replayability, and debugging support.
Permissions, policies, approval boundaries, or governance controls beyond basic sandboxing.
Plugins, MCP, hooks, custom tools, skills, or framework-level extension points.
Viability across local, self-hosted, cloud, CI, container, or remote execution targets.
Fit for software engineering tasks rather than generic chat only.
Usefulness for benchmarks, ablations, deterministic experiments, and explainable scaffolds.
